# Discussion Reply Templates

Use these templates to craft professional English replies to GitHub Discussions. Adjust placeholders like `@username`, `v1.2.x`, and `[Plugin Market Link]` as needed.

Discussions are community conversations, not bug reports. Keep replies warm, appreciative, and solution-oriented.

---

## 1. Idea / Feature Request — Implemented

Use when a user's idea has been shipped in a new version.

### Generic
- "Great news — this has been implemented in **v{version}**! 🎉\n\n{brief description of what was added}.\n\nUpdate the plugin to get this feature."

### With Details
- "Great news — this has been implemented in **v{version}**! 🎉\n\nThe plugin now provides {feature list}.\n\nAs you suggested, {reference to original suggestion}. Thanks for the detailed suggestion, it helped shape the implementation!"

---

## 2. Idea / Feature Request — Acknowledged

Use when you like the idea and plan to implement it, but haven't shipped yet.

- "Thanks for the thoughtful suggestion! I agree this would be a valuable addition. I'll look into implementing this in an upcoming release."
- "Great idea — I've noted this down. The implementation would involve {brief technical context}. I'll update this thread once it's ready."

---

## 3. Idea / Feature Request — Won't Fix / Out of Scope

Use when the idea doesn't fit the plugin's scope or architecture.

- "Thanks for the suggestion! After consideration, this falls outside the current scope of the plugin because {reason}. However, {alternative suggestion if any}."
- "I appreciate the idea, but this would conflict with {constraint}. I'll keep it in mind for future architecture changes."

---

## 4. Q&A — Answered

Use when answering a user's question.

- "Great question! {answer with technical details}.\n\nLet me know if you need more details."
- "Here's how this works: {explanation}. The relevant docs are at [Documentation Portal](https://fu-jie.github.io/openwebui-extensions/)."

---

## 5. Show and Tell — Acknowledged

Use when a user shares their setup, workflow, or creative use.

- "This is awesome! Thanks for sharing your setup. {specific comment about what impressed you}."
- "Love seeing this in action! {comment}. Would you mind if I featured this in the docs as an example?"

---

## 6. General — Follow-up

Use for general follow-up or status updates.

- "Just a quick update: {status}. I'll post again when there's more progress."
- "Following up on this — {resolution or current status}."

---

## 7. Pro-tip: Star Request

Gently handle star requests based on the user's current status.

### If User has NOT starred:
- "If you find this plugin helpful, a star on [openwebui-extensions](https://github.com/Fu-Jie/openwebui-extensions) would be much appreciated! ⭐"
- "We'd love your support! If this solved your problem, please consider starring the repository. ⭐"

### If User HAS already starred:
- "Thanks again for starring the project and for your continuous support!"
- "I appreciate your support and for being a stargazer of this project!"

---

## 8. Guiding to Official Market

Always provide the official market link when referencing plugin updates.

- "The update is now live! You can download the latest version from the official OpenWebUI Community page here: [Plugin Market Link]."
- "I recommend getting the updated version from the official store: [Link]."

---

## 9. Closing a Discussion (Mark as Answered / Resolved)

Use when the discussion topic is resolved. Note: Only mark Q&A discussions as "answered."

- "Glad this helped! Marking as answered. Feel free to continue the conversation if you have more questions."
- "This should be resolved now. Let me know if you run into any issues after updating."
